description: Add multi-tab targeting support so AI agents can discover all matching tabs for a plugin and dispatch tool calls to a specific tab by ID. Currently, when multiple tabs match a plugin (e.g., three Google Docs open), the extension auto-selects the 'best' one. This feature extends the wire protocol, state tracking, and dispatch pipeline to support explicit tab targeting while preserving the existing auto-select behavior as the default.

userStories.title: Extend shared wire types for multi-tab state and targeted dispatch
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the shared wire protocol types to support multiple tabs per plugin and optional tabId in tool dispatch, so that all layers can be updated incrementally against a stable contract.
acceptanceCriteria: TabSyncAllParams.tabs values change from { state, tabId, url } to { state, tabs: Array<{ tabId, url, title, ready }> } — each plugin reports an aggregate state plus a list of all matching tabs with per-tab readiness
TabStateChangedParams changes from { plugin, state, tabId, url } to { plugin, state, tabs: Array<{ tabId, url, title, ready }> } — same structure as sync
ToolDispatchParams adds optional tabId?: number field alongside existing plugin/tool/input
A new PluginTabInfo interface is defined: { tabId: number, url: string, title: string, ready: boolean }
All existing TabState ('closed'|'unavailable'|'ready') semantics are preserved — 'ready' means at least one tab is ready, 'unavailable' means tabs exist but none ready, 'closed' means no tabs
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Update extension tab state reporting to emit multi-tab payloads
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the browser extension to report all matching tabs per plugin (with per-tab readiness) in tab.syncAll and tab.stateChanged messages, so the MCP server has full visibility into every matching tab.
acceptanceCriteria: computePluginTabState returns { state: TabState, tabs: PluginTabInfo[] } — probes ALL matching tabs for readiness, not just the first ready one
sendTabSyncAll builds the new payload shape: each plugin maps to { state, tabs: [...] } with all matching tab IDs, URLs, titles, and readiness booleans
tab.stateChanged notifications use the new shape: { plugin, state, tabs: [...] }
lastKnownState cache is updated to store the full { state, tabs } so that diff detection works correctly (state changes OR tab list changes trigger notifications)
Tab title is obtained from chrome.tabs.Tab.title during findAllMatchingTabs or probeTabReadiness
Side panel forwarding of tab.stateChanged still works (side panel receives the new shape)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.ts and platform/browser-extension/src/tab-matching.ts.

Current flow:
1. computePluginTabState (tab-state.ts ~line 98) calls findAllMatchingTabs, iterates tabs, probes readiness, returns { state, tabId, url } for the FIRST ready tab
2. sendTabSyncAll (tab-state.ts ~line 138) calls computePluginTabState per plugin, builds Record<string, { state, tabId, url }>

New flow:
1. computePluginTabState must probe ALL matching tabs (not short-circuit on first ready), collect PluginTabInfo[] array with per-tab { tabId, url, title, ready }
2. Aggregate state: 'ready' if any tab ready, 'unavailable' if tabs exist but none ready, 'closed' if empty
3. Return { state, tabs: PluginTabInfo[] }
4. sendTabSyncAll and notifyAffectedPlugins must emit the new shape

findAllMatchingTabs (tab-matching.ts) already returns chrome.tabs.Tab objects with .title — just pass it through.

lastKnownState currently stores only TabState. It needs to store { state, tabs } (or at least a serialized representation) so that tab list changes (e.g., a new tab opened, or a tab's URL changed) trigger notifications even if the aggregate state didn't change. Consider using JSON.stringify comparison for the tabs array.

Also update the PluginTabStateInfo type in platform/browser-extension/src/extension-messages.ts to match the new shape.

The extension-commands.ts handleExtensionGetState (line ~30-83) should also be updated to report per-tab detail in the plugins array (it currently only shows aggregate tabState). Include the tabs array so extension_get_state shows all matching tabs.

userStories.title: Add tabId-targeted dispatch path in extension tool dispatch
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the extension's tool dispatch to accept an optional tabId in the tool.dispatch message, so tools can be executed on a specific tab when the AI agent provides one.
acceptanceCriteria: handleToolDispatch reads optional tabId from params and threads it through to the dispatch logic
When tabId is present: dispatch directly to that tab (no ranking, no fallback loop) — but MUST validate the tab's URL matches the plugin's URL patterns (security: prevent cross-origin abuse)
When tabId is absent: existing dispatchWithTabFallback behavior is preserved exactly (ranking + fallback)
If targeted tabId doesn't exist (chrome.tabs.get throws), return JSONRPC_NO_USABLE_TAB error with descriptive message

If targeted tabId's URL doesn't match plugin patterns, return a new error (JSONRPC_TAB_NOT_MATCHED or reuse JSONRPC_NO_USABLE_TAB) with a message explaining the tab doesn't match the plugin's URL patterns
If targeted tab's adapter is not ready, return JSONRPC_ADAPTER_NOT_READY (no fallback to other tabs — the agent asked for this specific tab)
Resource dispatch (handleResourceRead) and prompt dispatch (handlePromptGet) also support optional tabId with the same targeted-dispatch logic
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/browser-extension/src/tool-dispatch.ts, platform/browser-extension/src/dispatch-helpers.ts, and platform/browser-extension/src/resource-prompt-dispatch.ts.

The tabId arrives as part of the tool.dispatch JSON-RPC params (ToolDispatchParams now has optional tabId).

In handleToolDispatch (tool-dispatch.ts ~line 280):
- Extract tabId from params alongside plugin/tool/input
- If tabId is present, use a new targeted dispatch path instead of dispatchWithTabFallback

The targeted dispatch path (add to dispatch-helpers.ts or tool-dispatch.ts):
1. chrome.tabs.get(tabId) — if throws, tab doesn't exist, return error
2. urlMatchesPatterns(tab.url, plugin.urlPatterns) — if false, return error (security check)
3. Execute tool on that specific tab (same executeOnTab callback)
4. Return result directly — no fallback to other tabs

For the security check: this is critical. Without it, an AI agent could call slack_send_message with a tabId pointing to a banking tab, and the Slack adapter code would execute in the banking tab's context. The URL pattern check ensures the tab actually belongs to the plugin.

Also update dispatchWithTabFallback to optionally accept a tabId parameter and short-circuit to targeted dispatch. Or create a separate dispatchToTargetedTab function and have handleToolDispatch choose between them. Either approach works — pick whichever is cleaner.

Same pattern for handleResourceRead and handlePromptGet in resource-prompt-dispatch.ts — they also use dispatchWithTabFallback and should support tabId.

Update the unit tests in dispatch-helpers.test.ts to cover:
- Targeted dispatch succeeds when tab exists and URL matches
- Targeted dispatch fails when tab doesn't exist
- Targeted dispatch fails when tab URL doesn't match patterns
- Targeted dispatch fails (no fallback) when adapter not ready
- Omitting tabId preserves existing fallback behavior

userStories.title: Inject tabId into plugin tool schemas and strip before dispatch
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want every plugin tool's input schema to include an optional tabId parameter, so I can target a specific tab without plugin authors needing to add it themselves.
acceptanceCriteria: getEnabledToolsList injects an optional tabId property (type integer, description explaining it targets a specific tab) into a cloned copy of each plugin tool's input_schema — the original ManifestTool.input_schema is never mutated
The Ajv validator in buildRegistry is configured to accept the injected tabId — either by augmenting the schema before compilation, or by stripping tabId from args before validation
handlePluginToolCall extracts tabId from args before validation, threads it into the ToolDispatchParams sent to the extension, and does NOT pass tabId as part of the tool's input to the adapter
The tabId description in the schema mentions that browser_list_tabs or plugin_list_tabs can be used to discover valid tab IDs
Plugin tool handlers never see tabId in their input — it is a platform concern, not a plugin concern
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts, platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts, and platform/mcp-server/src/registry.ts.

The injection flow:

1. In getEnabledToolsList (mcp-setup.ts ~line 327), when iterating plugin tools, deep-clone toolDef.input_schema and inject:
   ```
   cloned.properties.tabId = { type: 'integer', description: 'Optional. Target a specific browser tab by its ID. When omitted, the platform automatically selects the best matching tab. Use browser_list_tabs or plugin_list_tabs to discover tab IDs.' }
   ```
   Do NOT add tabId to 'required' array. Push the cloned schema, not the original.

2. In handlePluginToolCall (mcp-tool-dispatch.ts ~line 112), BEFORE Ajv validation:
   - Extract tabId from args: `const tabId = typeof args.tabId === 'number' ? args.tabId : undefined`
   - Delete args.tabId (so the plugin's own schema validation doesn't see an unexpected property)
   - Validate the remaining args with the Ajv validator (which was compiled from the plugin's ORIGINAL schema)
   - Thread tabId into the dispatchToExtension call: `dispatchToExtension(state, 'tool.dispatch', { plugin, tool, input: args, tabId })`

This approach is the cleanest because:
- The Ajv validator uses the plugin's original schema (no augmentation needed)
- tabId is stripped before validation, so plugins with additionalProperties: false still work
- tabId is injected only in the MCP-facing schema (getEnabledToolsList), not in the stored manifest
- The extension receives tabId as a top-level dispatch param, not inside input

Alternative approach (augment Ajv schema) is also viable but adds complexity for no benefit since we can just strip before validation.

The dispatchToExtension call in handlePluginToolCall currently looks like:
  dispatchToExtension(state, 'tool.dispatch', { plugin: pluginName, tool: toolBaseName, input: args })
Change to:
  dispatchToExtension(state, 'tool.dispatch', { plugin: pluginName, tool: toolBaseName, input: args, ...(tabId !== undefined && { tabId }) })

userStories.title: Add plugin_list_tabs browser tool
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want a tool that lists all matching tabs for a plugin (with tab IDs, URLs, titles, and readiness), so I can discover which tabs are available before targeting one.
acceptanceCriteria: A new browser tool plugin_list_tabs is registered with input schema: { plugin?: string } — when plugin is provided, returns tabs for that plugin only; when omitted, returns tabs for all plugins
Response includes per-plugin entries with: pluginName, displayName, tabs array (each with tabId, url, title, ready boolean), and aggregate state
Tool is classified as 'observe' permission tier (auto-allowed, no confirmation needed)
Tool name is added to BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES in browser-tool-names.ts
Tool is exported from browser-tools/index.ts barrel
Extension handles the new browser.pluginListTabs dispatch method
When plugin param is provided but plugin doesn't exist, returns a clear error message
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Create platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/plugin-list-tabs.ts following the pattern of list-tabs.ts.

The tool definition:
```
name: 'plugin_list_tabs'
description: 'List open browser tabs that match a plugin\'s URL patterns. Returns tab IDs, URLs, titles, and readiness status for each matching tab. Use this to discover which tabs are available before targeting a specific one with the tabId parameter on plugin tools.'
input: z.object({ plugin: z.string().optional().describe('Plugin name. When provided, returns tabs for this plugin only. When omitted, returns tabs for all plugins.') })
```

The handler dispatches to the extension: dispatchToExtension(state, 'browser.pluginListTabs', { plugin: args.plugin })

In the extension, add the handler in message-router.ts methodHandlers:
- browser.pluginListTabs: reads all plugin metadata from storage, for each plugin (or just the requested one), calls findAllMatchingTabs to get matching tabs, probes each for readiness, returns structured response

Alternative server-side approach: the server already has tabMapping with the new multi-tab data from US-001/US-002. The tool could read directly from state.tabMapping instead of dispatching to the extension. This is simpler and avoids a round-trip. Use this approach if the data in tabMapping is fresh enough (it's updated via tab.syncAll and tab.stateChanged events). The response shape would be:
```
[{ plugin: 'googledocs', displayName: 'Google Docs', state: 'ready', tabs: [{ tabId: 123, url: 'https://docs.google.com/document/d/abc', title: 'My Doc', ready: true }, ...] }, ...]
```

Prefer the server-side approach (read from state.tabMapping) for simplicity and speed. Fall back to extension dispatch only if the data needs to be real-time probed.

Add the tool name to platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tool-names.ts. Add the export to platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts. Both files have bidirectional sync validation at module load time.

userStories.title: Unit tests for multi-tab state and targeted dispatch
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want comprehensive unit tests covering the new multi-tab state reporting and targeted dispatch paths, so regressions are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatch-helpers.test.ts has new tests for targeted dispatch: success case, tab-not-found, URL-mismatch (security), adapter-not-ready (no fallback), and that omitting tabId preserves existing behavior

tab-state tests cover: computePluginTabState returns all matching tabs with per-tab readiness, state transitions when tabs open/close, tab list changes trigger notifications even when aggregate state is unchanged
tab-matching.test.ts verifies findAllMatchingTabs returns tab titles
tool-dispatch.test.ts covers tabId extraction from params and threading to dispatch
resource-prompt-dispatch.test.ts covers tabId targeting for resource reads and prompt gets

Server-side tests: handleTabSyncAll parses new multi-tab payload correctly, handleTabStateChanged parses new payload, getEnabledToolsList injects tabId into plugin tool schemas without mutating originals, handlePluginToolCall strips tabId before validation and threads to dispatch
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Update existing test files and add new tests:

Extension unit tests:
- platform/browser-extension/src/dispatch-helpers.test.ts — add targeted dispatch tests (see US-003 notes for cases)
- platform/browser-extension/src/tab-matching.test.ts — verify title is returned
- platform/browser-extension/src/tool-dispatch.test.ts — tabId extraction
- platform/browser-extension/src/resource-prompt-dispatch.test.ts — tabId targeting

Server unit tests — find or create test files near the source:
- Test handleTabSyncAll with new payload shape (multi-tab arrays)
- Test handleTabStateChanged with new payload shape
- Test getEnabledToolsList schema injection (clone, not mutate)
- Test handlePluginToolCall tabId stripping and threading

Follow existing test patterns. Use vitest. Mock chrome.tabs.get, chrome.tabs.query, chrome.scripting.executeScript as the existing tests do.

For the schema injection test: call getEnabledToolsList, verify each plugin tool's inputSchema has a tabId property with type 'integer', verify the original ManifestTool.input_schema does NOT have tabId (proves cloning).

For the tabId stripping test: call handlePluginToolCall with args containing tabId, verify the Ajv validator receives args WITHOUT tabId, verify the dispatchToExtension call receives tabId as a top-level param.

userStories.title: E2E tests for multi-tab targeting
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want end-to-end tests that verify the full multi-tab targeting pipeline (MCP client → server → extension → specific tab → response), so the feature is validated across all layers.
acceptanceCriteria: New E2E test file e2e/multi-tab-targeting.e2e.ts with at least 5 test scenarios
Test 1: Open two tabs matching the same plugin, call plugin_list_tabs, verify both tabs are listed with correct URLs and tab IDs
Test 2: Open two tabs, call a plugin tool with tabId targeting the second tab, verify the tool executes on the correct tab (use a tool that returns tab-specific data like URL or title)
Test 3: Call a plugin tool with a tabId pointing to a non-matching tab (e.g., about:blank), verify it returns an error about URL not matching
Test 4: Call a plugin tool with a non-existent tabId, verify it returns a clean error
Test 5: Call a plugin tool WITHOUT tabId when multiple tabs are open, verify existing auto-select behavior still works (dispatches to best-ranked tab)
Test 6: Verify plugin_list_tabs returns readiness info — open a matching tab, wait for ready, verify ready:true in response
All tests use the existing fixture infrastructure (mcpServer, extensionContext, mcpClient, testServer)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Create e2e/multi-tab-targeting.e2e.ts following the patterns in e2e/dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts and e2e/tab-state-sync.e2e.ts.

Use the existing fixture infrastructure:
- mcpServer, testServer, extensionContext, mcpClient fixtures from e2e/fixtures.ts
- setupToolTest() from e2e/helpers.ts for standard preamble
- openTestAppTab() to open tabs matching the test plugin
- callToolExpectSuccess() for success assertions
- waitForToolResult() for polling

The test plugin (e2e-test-plugin) already has tools like 'echo' that return their input. For verifying which tab executed, you could:
1. Use browser_execute_script to set a unique marker on each tab's window object, then call a plugin tool that reads that marker
2. Or open two test app tabs with different paths/query params, and have the tool return window.location.href
3. The test app already supports different endpoints — open tab1 at testServer.url + '/page1' and tab2 at testServer.url + '/page2'

For plugin_list_tabs tests:
- Call mcpClient.callTool('plugin_list_tabs', {}) and parse the JSON response
- Call mcpClient.callTool('plugin_list_tabs', { plugin: 'e2e-test-plugin' }) for filtered view

For the non-matching tab test:
- Open a tab to about:blank or any URL not matching the test plugin's patterns
- Get its tabId via browser_list_tabs
- Call a plugin tool with that tabId, expect error

For the non-existent tabId test:
- Use tabId: 999999 (unlikely to exist)

Make sure to use waitForToolResult with polling for tests where tab state needs time to settle after opening.

userStories.title: Update documentation and CLAUDE.md files
userStories.description: As a platform developer and AI agent, I want documentation updated to explain multi-tab targeting, so the feature is discoverable and well-understood.
acceptanceCriteria: Root CLAUDE.md 'Key Concepts' section updated: mentions multi-tab targeting, tabId parameter injection, and the plugin_list_tabs tool

platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md updated: Tool Dispatch section explains tabId threading (strip from args, thread to extension), mentions the schema injection in getEnabledToolsList, documents the new plugin_list_tabs tool
platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md updated: Tab state machine section mentions multi-tab state reporting (tabs array per plugin), targeted dispatch path alongside the existing fallback path
platform/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md — no changes needed (plugin authors don't see tabId), but verify this is true
platform/shared/src/index.ts type JSDoc comments updated for the new/changed types (PluginTabInfo, TabSyncAllParams, TabStateChangedParams, ToolDispatchParams)
docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx updated: dispatch flow section mentions tabId targeting, tab state reporting shows multi-tab
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Documentation updates across multiple files:

1. Root CLAUDE.md (./CLAUDE.md):
   - In 'Key Concepts' section, add a bullet about multi-tab targeting: AI agents can target specific tabs using the optional tabId parameter on any plugin tool. The platform injects tabId into tool schemas automatically. Use plugin_list_tabs to discover available tabs.
   - Keep it concise — this file should stay high-level per its own guidelines.

2. platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md:
   - Tool Dispatch section: explain that handlePluginToolCall strips tabId from args before validation and threads it to the extension as a top-level ToolDispatchParams field. Mention that getEnabledToolsList injects an optional tabId property into each plugin tool's input_schema (cloned, not mutated).
   - Browser Tools section: mention plugin_list_tabs as a new tool for discovering matching tabs.

3. platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md:
   - Tab state machine: mention that tab.syncAll and tab.stateChanged now report arrays of tabs per plugin with per-tab readiness.
   - Dispatch: mention targeted dispatch (when tabId is present, dispatch directly to that tab with URL pattern validation; when absent, use existing ranking + fallback).

4. docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx:
   - Update the tool dispatch flow to mention optional tabId targeting
   - Update tab state description to mention multi-tab reporting

5. platform/shared/src/index.ts:
   - Add JSDoc to PluginTabInfo, TabSyncAllParams, TabStateChangedParams, ToolDispatchParams explaining the fields

6. Update e2e/helpers.ts BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES list to include 'plugin_list_tabs'
